skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
skill.summary: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to artifacts for consistent visual identity.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official skill from anthropics/skills repository with pre-approved content. Risk factor evidence shows references to network and external commands are documentation-only descriptions of font management and color application, not actual security concerns. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Apply Anthropic brand styling to presentations
content.value_statement: Creating on-brand presentations requires consistent application of colors and typography. This skill automates Anthropic's visual identity across all slides using official brand guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Apply Anthropic's official brand colors to presentation slides and artifacts
Set typography using Poppins for headings and Lora for body text
Use accent colors (orange, blue, green) for shapes and visual elements
Provide automatic font fallbacks to Arial and Georgia when custom fonts are unavailable
Ensure consistent color selection based on background contrast
limitations: Requires Poppins and Lora fonts to be pre-installed for optimal results
Designed primarily for PowerPoint presentations via python-pptx
Does not automatically install fonts on the user system
Best suited for slide-based artifacts rather than web or print media

use_cases.title: Corporate Presentation Creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams create on-brand PowerPoint presentations for external events and conferences.

use_cases.title: Internal Documentation Styling
use_cases.description: Technical writers apply consistent brand formatting to internal training materials and reports.

use_cases.title: Design System Compliance
use_cases.description: Designers ensure all presentation outputs meet company visual identity standards.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brand Color Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply Anthropic brand colors to this presentation using the official color palette. Use Dark (#141413) for text, Light (#faf9f5) for backgrounds, and accent colors for visual elements.

prompt_templates.title: Typography Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Style this presentation with Anthropic typography guidelines. Apply Poppins font to all headings 24pt and larger, and Lora font to body text. Include Arial and Georgia as fallbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Brand Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply complete Anthropic brand styling to this presentation including all brand colors, typography settings, and accent color cycling for shapes. Ensure smart color selection based on background contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Visual Identity Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement Anthropic's full visual identity system across this multi-slide presentation. Apply the complete color palette with appropriate hierarchy, configure typography with proper fallbacks, cycle accent colors through shapes and non-text elements, and maintain visual consistency throughout all slides.

output_examples.input: A blank PowerPoint presentation with placeholder text
output_examples.output: A fully styled presentation with Anthropic brand colors applied to backgrounds and text, Poppins headings, Lora body text, and accent-colored shapes for visual interest.

output_examples.input: Raw slide content with unformatted text boxes
output_examples.output: Professionally formatted slides with proper color contrast, consistent typography hierarchy, and on-brand accent colors for shapes and graphical elements.

best_practices: Install Poppins and Lora fonts on your system before using this skill for optimal results
Use brand colors consistently across all presentation elements to maintain visual identity
Test presentations on different displays to ensure color accuracy and readability
anti_patterns: Using non-brand colors that conflict with Anthropic's visual identity guidelines
Applying brand fonts without fallback options for systems without font installation
Overusing accent colors which can dilute their visual impact and brand consistency

faq.question: What fonts do I need installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: For optimal results, install Poppins for headings and Lora for body text. The skill includes fallbacks to Arial and Georgia if these fonts are not available.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-PowerPoint artifacts?
faq.answer: This skill is designed primarily for PowerPoint presentations using python-pptx. The brand guidelines can be applied manually to other formats using the documented color values and typography settings.

faq.question: What are the official Anthropic brand colors?
faq.answer: Main colors include Dark (#141413), Light (#faf9f5), Mid Gray (#b0aea5), and Light Gray (#e8e6dc). Accent colors are Orange (#d97757), Blue (#6a9bcc), and Green (#788c5d).

faq.question: How does the skill handle systems without brand fonts?
faq.answer: The skill automatically falls back to Arial for headings and Georgia for body text when Poppins and Lora are not installed, ensuring readability across all systems.

faq.question: Can I customize the accent color cycling order?
faq.answer: The skill cycles through orange, blue, and green accents by default. For custom ordering, you would need to modify the skill configuration or apply colors manually.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for web-based presentations?
faq.answer: This skill is optimized for PowerPoint via python-pptx. For web presentations, apply the same color values and font families in your CSS using the documented brand guidelines.
